reasoning: The informal statement asserts that the average velocity over [2, 2.5], namely (y(2.5) - y(2))/(2.5 - 2), equals -32 ft/s. The Lean lemma only asserts that heightFunction 2 = 16, which is merely one intermediate sub-computation needed to derive the full claim. It does not compute y(2.5), nor does it form the difference quotient, nor does it claim the average velocity equals -32. The Lean statement captures only a small piece of what the informal statement claims, so it is strictly weaker than the intended claim.
